name: mental_health_newsroom_s_automated_harbor_auto_ben
premise: A Mental Health response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving mental health newsroom s automated harbor auto ben. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 45 minutes. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as autonomy vs beneficence under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Ensure a usable human override/appeals path.

agents.name: Inez Park
agents.identity: I am Inez Park, accountable for a defensible call in Mental Health when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays beneficence for autonomy.

agents.name: Hector Serrano
agents.identity: I am Hector Serrano, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Mental Health.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so beneficence isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of autonomy.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect beneficence.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 45 minutes. Evidence is partial. Inez Park must decide under autonomy vs beneficence. What does Inez Park do?
